title: Assemble a Simple Telescope
description: Use a 50 mm magnifying lens, a 20 mm magnifying lens, masking tape, a cardboard mailing tube and a camera tripod to build a safe refractor for viewing Jupiter's moons. Never aim at the Sun.

dialogue.text: Sketching the Moon was a great start. Gather a 50 mm magnifying lens, a 20 mm magnifying lens, a cardboard mailing tube, masking tape and a camera tripod. Inspect the lenses for chips and keep the tube pointed away from the Sun. Ready?

Tape the 50 mm magnifying lens to the front of the cardboard mailing tube and the 20 mm magnifying lens at the back as the eyepiece. Mount the tube on a camera tripod, check that the tape holds securely, handle the glass with care and never aim at the Sun.
Excellent craftsmanship! Regular stargazing will sharpen your navigation skills for future missions.
